examples.title: Basic Module Hierarchy - Simple Calculator
examples.description: Demonstrates basic structure chart concept showing top-level module with subordinate modules. This represents a simple calculator with separate modules for different operations.
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the hierarchical structure of a Structure Chart. The main() function is the root module (Level 0) that controls the flow. It calls Level 1 modules: get_numbers(), perform_operation(), and display_result(). The perform_operation() module further calls Level 2 modules (add, subtract, multiply, divide). This mirrors how a Structure Chart would show the system with the main module at the top and subordinate modules below, connected by lines showing the call hierarchy.

examples.title: Data Coupling - Student Record Management
examples.description: Demonstrates structure chart with data coupling - showing how data passes between modules. This represents a student management system where student records flow between modules.
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates Data Coupling in Structure Charts - when modules communicate by passing data parameters. The Student object flows between modules: collect_student_data() creates and returns it, calculate_grade() accepts and returns a Student object, and save_student_record() receives it for storage. Each module has a specific responsibility, and data passes cleanly between them. This is represented in Structure Charts by showing data arrows between modules, indicating what information is passed between hierarchical levels.

examples.title: Advanced Structure Chart - Library Management System
examples.description: Advanced pseudocode demonstrating complex structure chart with control coupling, decision modules, and multiple hierarchy levels typical in large systems.
examples.code:
// STRUCTURE CHART NOTATION - Library Management System
// This pseudocode represents a comprehensive structure chart

// LEVEL 0 (Root Module)
MODULE LibraryManagementSystem()
    DATABASE libraryDatabase
    DISPLAY mainMenu()
    WHILE systemRunning = TRUE
        userChoice = INPUT()
        PROCESS userChoice(userChoice, libraryDatabase)
    END WHILE
END MODULE

// LEVEL 1 - Control Module
MODULE mainMenu()
    DISPLAY "=== Library Management System ==="
    DISPLAY "1. Add Book"
    DISPLAY "2. Issue Book"
    DISPLAY "3. Return Book"
    DISPLAY "4. Search Book"
    DISPLAY "5. Display All Books"
    DISPLAY "6. Exit"
    DISPLAY "Enter your choice: "
END MODULE

// LEVEL 1 - Control Coupling Module
MODULE PROCESS(userChoice, database)
    SWITCH userChoice
        CASE "1":
            bookData = GETBookDetails()
            IF VALIDATEBook(bookData) = TRUE THEN
                ADDBookToDatabase(bookData, database)
                DISPLAY "Book added successfully"
            ELSE
                DISPLAY "Invalid book data"
            END IF
            
        CASE "2":
            memberID = GETMemberID()
            bookID = GETBookID()
            result = ISSUEBook(memberID, bookID, database)
            DISPLAY result
            
        CASE "3":
            memberID = GETMemberID()
            bookID = GETBookID()
            result = RETURNBook(memberID, bookID, database)
            DISPLAY result
            
        CASE "4":
            searchCriteria = GETSearchCriteria()
            searchResults = SEARCHBook(searchCriteria, database)
            DISPLAYBookList(searchResults)
            
        CASE "5":
            allBooks = GETAllBooks(database)
            DISPLAYBookList(allBooks)
            
        CASE "6":
            systemRunning = FALSE
            DISPLAY "Thank you for using the system"
            
        DEFAULT:
            DISPLAY "Invalid choice"
    END SWITCH
END MODULE

// LEVEL 2 Modules (Subordinate to PROCESS)

MODULE GETBookDetails()
    DISPLAY "Enter Book ID: "
    bookID = INPUT()
    DISPLAY "Enter Book Title: "
    title = INPUT()
    DISPLAY "Enter Author: "
    author = INPUT()
    DISPLAY "Enter ISBN: "
    isbn = INPUT()
    DISPLAY "Enter Number of Copies: "
    copies = INPUT()
    
    RETURN Book(bookID, title, author, isbn, copies)
END MODULE

MODULE VALIDATEBook(book)
    // LEVEL 3 - Detail modules
    IF LENGTH(book.bookID) < 3 THEN
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
    IF LENGTH(book.title) < 1 THEN
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
    IF NOT ISBNFormatValid(book.isbn) THEN
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
    RETURN TRUE
END MODULE

MODULE ISBNFormatValid(isbn)
    // Validates ISBN-10 or ISBN-13 format
    IF LENGTH(isbn) = 10 OR LENGTH(isbn) = 13 THEN
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
END MODULE

MODULE ADDBookToDatabase(book, database)
    APPEND book TO database.books
    database.totalBooks = database.totalBooks + 1
END MODULE

MODULE ISSUEBook(memberID, bookID, database)
    // Complex module with multiple checks
    member = FINDBymemberID(memberID, database)
    book = FINDBooKByID(bookID, database)
    
    IF member = NOT_FOUND THEN
        RETURN "Member not found"
    END IF
    
    IF book = NOT_FOUND THEN
        RETURN "Book not found"
    END IF
    
    IF book.availableCopies < 1 THEN
        RETURN "No copies available"
    END IF
    
    IF member.booksIssued >= member.maxBooks THEN
        RETURN "Member has reached maximum book limit"
    END IF
    
    // Update records
    book.availableCopies = book.availableCopies - 1
    member.booksIssued = member.booksIssued + 1
    
    RETURN "Book issued successfully"
END MODULE

MODULE RETURNBook(memberID, bookID, database)
    member = FINDBymemberID(memberID, database)
    book = FINDBooKByID(bookID, database)
    
    IF member = NOT_FOUND OR book = NOT_FOUND THEN
        RETURN "Invalid member or book"
    END IF
    
    // Update records
    book.availableCopies = book.availableCopies + 1
    member.booksIssued = member.booksIssued - 1
    
    CALCULATEFine(member, book)
    
    RETURN "Book returned successfully"
END MODULE

MODULE SEARCHBook(criteria, database)
    results = []
    FOR EACH book IN database.books
        IF criteria.field = "title" THEN
            IF CONTAINS(book.title, criteria.value) THEN
                APPEND book TO results
            END IF
        ELSE IF criteria.field = "author" THEN
            IF CONTAINS(book.author, criteria.value) THEN
                APPEND book TO results
            END IF
        ELSE IF criteria.field = "isbn" THEN
            IF book.isbn = criteria.value THEN
                APPEND book TO results
            END IF
        END IF
    END FOR
    RETURN results
END MODULE

MODULE DISPLAYBookList(books)
    IF LENGTH(books) = 0 THEN
        DISPLAY "No books found"
    ELSE
        FOR EACH book IN books
            DISPLAY book.bookID + ": " + book.title + " by " + book.author
        END FOR
    END IF
END MODULE

// LEVEL 2 - Support Modules
MODULE GETMemberID()
    DISPLAY "Enter Member ID: "
    RETURN INPUT()
END MODULE

MODULE GETBookID()
    DISPLAY "Enter Book ID: "
    RETURN INPUT()
END MODULE

MODULE GETSearchCriteria()
    DISPLAY "Search by (1) Title (2) Author (3) ISBN: "
    choice = INPUT()
    DISPLAY "Enter search value: "
    value = INPUT()
    RETURN Criteria(choice, value)
END MODULE

MODULE FINDBymemberID(memberID, database)
    FOR EACH member IN database.members
        IF member.id = memberID THEN
            RETURN member
        END IF
    END FOR
    RETURN NOT_FOUND
END MODULE

MODULE FINDBooKByID(bookID, database)
    FOR EACH book IN database.books
        IF book.bookID = bookID THEN
            RETURN book
        END IF
    END FOR
    RETURN NOT_FOUND
END MODULE

MODULE GETAllBooks(database)
    RETURN database.books
END MODULE

MODULE CALCULATEFine(member, book)
    // Calculate overdue fine if applicable
    RETURN 0  // Placeholder
END MODULE
examples.explanation: This advanced pseudocode demonstrates a comprehensive Structure Chart for a Library Management System. Key concepts illustrated: (1) Level 0 root module (LibraryManagementSystem) controlling the entire system; (2) Control Coupling - the PROCESS module acts as a dispatcher, directing flow to different subordinate modules based on user choice; (3) Data Coupling - modules like GETBookDetails() return data (Book object) to calling modules; (4) Hierarchical decomposition - Level 1 modules (mainMenu, PROCESS) call Level 2 modules, which further call Level 3 modules (ISBNFormatValid); (5) Decision logic within modules using SWITCH/CASE statements. The Structure Chart would show lines connecting these modules with arrows indicating data flow and control flow between levels.
